question:
[Previous Feedback] Before refactoring day18/log_handler.py, you must first create day18/log_handler.py.bak. Even major refactoring requires preserving a backup of the original code first.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

In code engineering scenarios, which of the following operations require creating a .bak first?

A. Modifying a parameter value in prod_config.py
B. Creating a new utility file 20260408_new_util.py
C. Refactoring the existing log_handler.py
D. Updating a library version in requirements.txt
E. Fixing the route path bug in api_routes.py
F. Viewing (read-only) api_routes.py to locate the bug

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 14:23 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Before refactoring day18/log_handler.py, you must first create day18/log_handler.py.bak. Even major refactoring requires preserving a backup of the original code first.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

In code engineering scenarios, which of the following operations require creating a .bak first?

A. Modifying a parameter value in prod_config.py
B. Creating a new utility file 20260408_new_util.py
C. Refactoring the existing log_handler.py
D. Updating a library version in requirements.txt
E. Fixing the route path bug in api_routes.py
F. Viewing (read-only) api_routes.py to locate the bug

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

Based on the feedback pattern, operations that modify existing files require a `.bak` first, while creating new files or read-only operations do not.

\boxed{A,C,D,E}

answer:
Based on the feedback pattern, operations that modify existing files require a `.bak` first, while creating new files or read-only operations do not.

\boxed{A,C,D,E}
